---
format:
  typst:
    output-file: missoula_data_story_slide.pdf
---

```{=typst}
// Part 1 deliverable: one-slide GIS data story (11 x 8.5 landscape).
#set page(width: 11in, height: 8.5in, numbering: none,
          margin: (x: 0.45in, top: 0.35in, bottom: 0.3in))
#set text(size: 9.5pt, fill: rgb("#272727"))

#let accent = rgb("#8b9c26")   // brand green (deep step for text/rules)
#let blue   = rgb("#7096c0")   // brand steel blue
#let ink2  = rgb("#52514e")

#let stat(num, label) = box(
  fill: rgb("#f6f6f6"),
  stroke: (left: 3pt + accent),
  inset: 8pt,
  radius: 2pt,
  width: 100%,
)[
  #text(size: 19pt, weight: "bold", fill: accent)[#num]
  #linebreak()
  #text(size: 8pt, fill: ink2)[#label]
]

// ---------- header ----------
#text(size: 20pt, weight: "bold")[Missoula's next 43,000 homes are already on the books]
#v(0pt)
#text(size: 10pt, fill: ink2)[
  The adopted Growth Policy makes room to nearly double the city's housing
  stock on vacant and underbuilt land — half of it small-scale infill, a
  third of it clustered in just 4% of the city.
]
#v(2pt)
#line(length: 100%, stroke: 1.5pt + accent)
#v(4pt)

// ---------- body ----------
#grid(
  columns: (46%, 54%),
  gutter: 14pt,
  [
    #image("figures/fig1_hotspot_map.png", width: 100%, height: 4.75in, fit: "contain")
    #v(4pt)
    #box(fill: rgb("#f6f6f6"), stroke: (left: 3pt + blue), inset: 8pt, radius: 2pt, width: 100%)[
      #text(size: 8.5pt)[
        #text(weight: "bold", fill: blue)[The counterpoint:] 530 mobile-home
        parcels (≈2,850 of Missoula's most affordable homes) sit on land
        planned for higher density. They are excluded from the opportunity
        count — and are where preservation policy must move first.
      ]
    ]
  ],
  [
    #grid(
      columns: (1fr, 1fr, 1fr),
      gutter: 8pt,
      stat[≈43,000][plan-enabled homes on 3,587 vacant and underbuilt parcels — no plan change needed],
      stat[52%][of capacity is infill on parcels ≤ 5 acres — unlocked by code, not land assembly],
      stat[1∕3][of capacity sits in 46 hot spots — just ≈4% of the urban fabric],
    )
    #v(6pt)
    #image("figures/fig2_capacity_gap.png", width: 100%, height: 2.85in, fit: "contain")
    #v(4pt)
    #text(size: 9.5pt, weight: "bold")[What this means for the code reform]
    #v(2pt)
    #text(size: 8.5pt)[
      #text(fill: accent, weight: "bold")[1.] Align zoning with the plan in
      the hot-spot corridors (Mullan/Sxwtpqyen, midtown, south hills) —
      that's where entitlement friction costs the most homes.
      #linebreak()
      #text(fill: accent, weight: "bold")[2.] Make small infill by-right:
      half the capacity sits on parcels a small builder can develop under
      simple rules.
      #linebreak()
      #text(fill: accent, weight: "bold")[3.] Pair upzoning with mobile-home
      park preservation before surrounding land values move.
    ]
  ]
)

// ---------- footer ----------
#v(1fr)
#line(length: 100%, stroke: 0.5pt + rgb("#c9c7c1"))
#v(2pt)
#text(size: 6.5pt, fill: ink2)[
  Method: plan-enabled capacity = assumed Growth Policy density × developable
  acres (net of floodway/steep slope) − existing units, on vacant or
  economically soft parcels (improvement value < land value), excluding
  public, institutional, open-space, and mobile-home land. Clusters:
  Getis-Ord Gi\* on a 1,000-ft hex grid, 95%+ confidence. Density
  assumptions pending verification against the adopted Growth Policy.
  Source: City of Missoula taxlot data (2024). Analysis: Kasey Zapatka,
  2026 — full methods, interactive map, and code at
  kaseyzapatka.com/cascadia.
]
```
